metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the detection vocabulary for each named recall pattern (ui-artifact leakage, domain-mismatch, prompt/contract/output-schema drift): what constitutes a match, what the FP boundary condition is, and at least one positive and one negative example per pattern, co-located in the plan or a referenced spec.
findings.finding: The three recall-pattern categories named in the plan's 'What' section — 'ui-artifact leakage', 'domain-mismatch (reimplementing an existing prompt-library entry)', and 'prompt/contract/output-schema drift on rebar's own LLM surfaces' — are referenced but their value vocabulary (what constitutes a match, what the boundary condition is, what differentiates a true positive from a false positive for each) is never defined anywhere in the plan or in the current overlay file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete file path, `prompt:` id, and minimum dataset structure (at least one recall case and one no-fire case per named pattern) for the llm-prompts overlay eval spec, and confirm it is resolvable by load_eval_spec.
findings.finding: The eval spec + fixtures for the llm-prompts overlay (AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval') do not exist in the repo and have no concrete file path specified in the plan; the eval infra resolves specs at a fixed path (`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/<prompt-id>.eval.yaml` or `.rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml`) and raises EvalError when absent.
findings.location: Plan 'Files' section (second artifact); src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ (directory listing); src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:76-80
findings.suggested_fix: Name the file `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml` (matching the packaged eval-spec naming convention), set `prompt: code-review-llm-prompts`, and include at least one `expect: finding` and one `expect: pass` dataset case per named recall pattern.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the co-location protocol for FP-discrimination guards within the overlay prompt: specify whether each guard immediately follows its paired recall pattern (inline), appears in a named section, or uses a standard negation phrasing — and confirm this is consistent with the pink-elephant anti-pattern guidance.
findings.finding: The FP-discrimination guards are referenced in the plan ('FP-guards (both halves)') but no decision rule or co-location protocol is specified: the plan does not say where in the overlay prompt the FP-guard instructions should appear relative to the recall-pattern instructions, nor what structure they must follow (inline guard clauses, a separate section, a pink-elephant-style negation, etc.).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to the plan explaining WHY FP-discrimination guards are encoded in the overlay prompt body (vs. expressed only as eval no-fire fixtures), and state the risk trade-off (prompt-side guards constrain the live reviewer but can suppress recall; eval-side fixtures only measure; the plan needs both, but the prompt-body encoding of guards should be scoped to avoid blunting recall).
findings.finding: The plan states no positive rationale for WHY recall patterns and FP guards are co-located in the same overlay prompt (the internal mechanism choice), as distinct from why the overlay boundary (not the verifier) is correct. The 'locked principle' explains the boundary decision but not the internal design — a reader cannot judge whether inline co-location is better than, say, a structured criteria-routing approach or a dedicated FP sub-prompt.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the target file path for the llm-prompts overlay eval spec (e.g. `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml`) to the Files section, and confirm the eval runner can discover and execute code-review overlay evals from that location.
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' but no eval spec or fixture file for the llm-prompts code-review overlay exists anywhere in the repo, and the plan's 'Files' section does not specify a target path for this artifact — making the eval deliverable unverifiable and unanchored.
findings.location: Files section / AC2: 'eval spec + fixtures for the llm-prompts overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml` to the Files section. Model its structure after `plan-review-finder.eval.yaml` (recall + no-fire labeled cases). Confirm the eval runner's discovery path covers code-review eval specs alongside plan-review ones.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a concrete pass threshold for the eval (e.g., 'all N recall fixtures fire, 0 of M no-fire fixtures trigger') and a minimum fixture count per pattern category in AC2.
findings.finding: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' defines no numeric threshold (e.g., 3/3 recall cases fire, 0/N no-fire cases trigger), so an agent cannot determine whether the eval result constitutes 'passing' without guessing.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC2 as: 'Eval suite covers ≥1 fixture per recall pattern (≥3 total) and ≥1 no-fire fixture per guard (≥3 total); eval run produces 3/3 recall hits and 0/3 false positives with zero regressions on existing overlays.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete file path for the new eval spec (expected: `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml`) so the `load_eval_spec` resolver and offline CI discipline job pick it up automatically.
findings.finding: The second file artifact — 'eval spec + fixtures for the llm-prompts overlay' — has no concrete file path specified in the plan, and no such file exists anywhere in the repo (no `code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml` in `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`, `.rebar/evals/`, or elsewhere). The plan names it as a deliverable but leaves the path implicit, making it an under-specified edit target.
findings.location: Files section: 'eval spec + fixtures for the llm-prompts overlay'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml` to the Files section as the explicit path for the eval spec artifact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC2 (e.g., the CLI invocation that runs the llm-prompts eval suite and the expected stdout/exit-code that constitutes a pass).
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is specified for AC2; the plan lists 'eval spec + fixtures' as a file to create but never states how to execute the eval or what command/output constitutes evidence of passage.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the eval spec whether recall and no-fire cases are validated via live (or recorded-replay) LLM invocations rather than purely offline pattern matching, so the eval cannot go green while the overlay is ineffective at the real LLM boundary.
findings.finding: The plan specifies 'eval spec + fixtures' for the llm-prompts overlay but does not clarify whether the eval actually invokes the live LLM or uses an offline/canned substitute. Because the overlay's behavior is LLM-mediated (the overlay text influences live LLM scoring), an offline-only fixture suite that never calls the real LLM would bypass the actual boundary, letting the eval go green while the overlay is silently ignored or mis-parsed by the model.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 / Files: eval spec + fixtures for the llm-prompts overlay
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or note specifying that at least a representative subset of recall and no-fire fixtures are run through the actual LLM reviewer (or a faithful recorded-replay that captures real LLM output), not solely through static text assertions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cite the ADR, doc section, or code comment that establishes the 'domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier' constraint (or add a brief note in the plan linking to the authoritative source).
findings.finding: The plan invokes a 'locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier' as a design constraint justifying the work, but no ADR, doc section, or code comment in the repo uses this phrase or cites this principle — the assertion is unverifiable from the codebase.
findings.location: What section: 'per the locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan how the live model eval for the new `code-review-llm-prompts` eval spec will be run and certified (manual dispatch, existing workflow, or new workflow), since no code-review overlay eval CI workflow currently exists.
findings.finding: The second deliverable (the eval spec file) is a CREATION — a net-new artifact that does not exist. The plan treats it as a peer edit target alongside the existing `code-review-llm-prompts.md`, but it is new behavior. This creation is intentional and in-scope for the plan's goals, but the plan does not acknowledge that the eval harness/CI for code-review overlays (as distinct from plan-review overlays) must be able to discover and run this new spec — the `eval.yml` offline discipline job validates packaged specs, but there is currently no analog of the plan-review eval CI workflow (`eval-plan-review-container.yml`) for the code-review overlay eval.
findings.location: Files section / AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
